expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid banding scenarios distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states the required case focus under “Benchmark intent” and reiterates it in the acceptance checklist: “Modern Grid banding scenarios that clearly distinguish: Styling variants…, Interactions…, Backward-compatible rendering outcomes…”. It further enumerates concrete scenario areas: “Banding colors / styling variants…”, “Apply banding by header scope…”, and “Backward-compatible rendering outcomes… verify parity with 'Report'…”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: Both result.md and execution_notes.md state that the phase4a deliverable artifact is missing and compliance cannot be confirmed: “Determination: Cannot confirm phase4a deliverable exists / is compliant” and “no Phase 4a output artifact… is provided, so phase4a alignment… cannot be verified.” While it quotes phase4a structure requirements, it does not actually provide a phase4a subcategory-only draft, so alignment is not demonstrated.
